text:
Hugo Hay says he ‘understands’ the anger from George Mills after their bust-up at the end of the 5000m Olympic qualifier on Wednesday.

Mills angrily confronted Hay over the finish lineas a collision with his French rival caused him to fall on the final street of the first 5000m heat at the Stade de France.

The Team GB runner, who is the son of former England andLeeds Uniteddefender Danny Mills, also took three athletes down with him after unsuccessfully jostling for position with Hay.

Following an appeal, Mills has now advanced to Saturday’s final following a review by the video referee.

But Hay, who will also be in the final, maintains his innocence and claims he was unaware of the contact with Mills during the closing stages of the race.

‘I don’t know what happened. I didn’t feel anything,’ said Hay.

‘I was surprised that George came to see me after the race, because I didn’t feel anything, well, no more than usual.

‘It pushed in the last 400m, it was really a massacre. I turned around, there was no one left. It was incredible. I have no idea what’s going to happen, it scares me.

‘For me, it happened clearly. That’s what I said to George. If I’m guilty, sorry, but I think I was pushed. I don’t think I did anything wrong, but it scares me a little bit.

‘I hope it works out and that I can get into the final. If the protest ever goes through and I’m eliminated, of course, it will hurt but I know that I had the level to qualify and that I’ve never been so strong. For me, I am qualified, that is the most important thing.’

Hay also reiterated that he would apologise to Mills if he felt the footage proved he was in the wrong over the collision.

‘We are at very high speeds, so of course, the slightest contact throws you off balance,’ Hay said.

‘I was pushed two years ago and I was really angry after the line. That’s what I told the others, I understand George’s reaction, of course. There’s the adrenaline, the disappointment. He was very angry, he said a few sweet words to me and that it was because of me that they had fallen.

‘He told me: ‘No, I don’t care what you say’. I told him it was normal for him to react like that. I told him I understood and that we would discuss it afterwards. And that I would apologise if I am guilty, but I don’t have that impression.’

When shown the footage of the incident after the race, Hay said: ‘Oh yes, I’m being pushed! Oh lalala! No, for me George is coming at me.’

A furious Mills was asked by French media for his verdict and said: ‘You know very well what happened.’

title: Hugo Hay reveals what George Mills said during row after 5000m Olympic heat | Metro News
description: Hugo Hay says he 'understands' the anger from George Mills after their bust-up at the end of the 5000m Olympic qualifier on Wednesday.
